passage: A clinical trial compared the effectiveness of three different treatments for chronic migraines over a 12-week period. The lead researcher concluded that Treatment B was the most effective option, producing the greatest reduction in monthly migraine episodes.
stem: Which choice most effectively uses data from the figure to support the researcher's conclusion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Patients receiving Treatment B experienced an average reduction of 8.4 episodes per month, compared to 5.1 for Treatment A and 6.3 for Treatment C.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Patients receiving Treatment C experienced an average reduction of 6.3 episodes per month, which was 1.2 episodes more than Treatment A's reduction of 5.1.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Patients in the placebo group experienced an average reduction of 2.0 episodes per month, while Treatment A patients saw a reduction of 5.1 episodes.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Treatment B patients started with an average of 14.2 monthly episodes and ended with 5.8, while Treatment A patients started with 13.8 and ended with 8.7.

explanation: The claim is that Treatment B was most effective with the greatest reduction. Choice A directly compares all three treatments' reductions (B: 8.4, A: 5.1, C: 6.3), clearly showing B had the largest reduction. Choice B compares Treatment C to A without mentioning B. Choice C compares placebo to A without addressing B. Choice D provides raw numbers for B and A but requires calculation and omits Treatment C.

distractorRationale.A: Correct: directly compares all three treatments' reduction values, confirming Treatment B's 8.4 is the highest.
distractorRationale.B: Wrong (misread value): compares Treatment C to Treatment A but omits Treatment B, which is the subject of the claim.
distractorRationale.C: Wrong (irrelevant data): compares the placebo group to Treatment A, neither of which is the claimed best treatment.
distractorRationale.D: Wrong (incomplete evidence): shows raw start/end values for B and A only, requiring the reader to calculate reductions and missing Treatment C entirely.

imageDescription: Bar chart titled 'Average Reduction in Monthly Migraine Episodes by Treatment (12-Week Trial).' X-axis: Treatment Group (Placebo, Treatment A, Treatment B, Treatment C). Y-axis: Average Reduction in Episodes per Month (0-10). Bars: Placebo = 2.0, Treatment A = 5.1, Treatment B = 8.4, Treatment C = 6.3. Source: National Headache Research Consortium, 2024.
